title: Effect of trench-outer rise bending-related faulting on seismic Poisson's ratio and mantle anisotropy: a case study offshore of Southern Central Chile
abstract: Joint inversion of seismic refraction and wide-angle reflection data at the Southern Central Chile outer rise yields the P- and S-wave velocity structure of the incoming oceanic Nazca Plate. The Poisson's ratio in the uppermost mantle increases from ~1.75 in the outer rise to values of >1.8 at the trench axis, indicating increasing serpentinization. Bending-related faulting opens near-vertical cracks modifying porosity and permeability and providing pathways for seawater.

location.description: Outer rise of the Nazca plate offshore southern central Chile (~43°S), between Chiloe and Guafo Fracture Zones; TIPTEQ cruise SO181

data.name: SPOC/TIPTEQ OBS P- and S-wave Wide-Angle Data — South Chile Outer Rise
data.description: Wide-angle P- and S-wave OBS refraction data from the outer rise of southern central Chile used to derive 2D Poisson's ratio models; documents increasing serpentinization toward the trench. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — South Chile Outer Rise
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SO161 to map bending-related fault scarps and outer rise topography. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: SPOC (SO161) and TIPTEQ (SO181) data accessible via MGDS. Both P and S waves used to derive Vp/Vs (Poisson's ratio) structure.

processing.summary: Joint P- and S-wave seismic refraction data from OBS instruments deployed at the outer rise of southern central Chile (SPOC/TIPTEQ cruises) were processed to derive 2D Vp, Vs and Poisson's ratio models. Travel-time modeling reveals systematic Poisson's ratio increase toward the trench axis reflecting progressive serpentinization. Anisotropy analysis indicates bending-related normal faults create subvertical cracks enabling seawater infiltration.
workflow: 1. OBS data processing and instrument relocation
processing.notes: S-wave data from converted phases on OBS; first study to derive Poisson's ratio at this outer rise location

analysis_notes: PDF validated 2026-04-05. Corrected: year_acquired 2001→2004 (TIPTEQ cruise SO181, 2004); source_volume_cui null→3905 (8×8L G-guns = 64L); vessel→'R/V SONNE SO181 (TIPTEQ)'; seismic lines updated to P01a (65 km E-W) and P03 (76 km N-S); software RAYINVR→TOMO2D + Reflectivity method; bounding box narrowed to ~43°S (Chiloe-Guafo FZ area). No data availability statement found.
